source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two perpendicular non-conducting rods carry uniform charges \( +2.50\,\mu\text{C} \) and \( -2.50\mu\text{C} \), respectively, along their lengths. Find the magnitude of the electric field these rods produce at point \( P \), which is 60.0\text{cm} from each rod.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false directly indicate the problem is intended to rely on the figure
The text references a geometric setup ('two perpendicular rods' and point P relative to each rod) that is likely clarified by the image
A primary image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual component is available and part of the sample
multi_step: Solving likely requires finding the electric field contribution from each charged rod separately
The two rod contributions then need to be combined using geometry/vector reasoning because the rods are perpendicular and carry opposite charges
This is more than direct lookup, but still a fairly standard physics derivation with a single final numeric target
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric result and unit makes correctness checkable
There are 4 multiple-choice options, so the final answer can be validated against a closed set
The problem is a standard physics computation with deterministic inputs, and registry_passed=true indicates the assets are intact

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and primary image are all present, and asset registry passed with no reported issues.
The problem is strongly image-dependent, and the required figure is available, so the core geometry is recoverable.
Initial scoring is strong overall: high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning value, and high verifiability.
Risk flags do not block inclusion because the geometry underspecification concern is mitigated by the existing image, and answer-format variation is manageable.
